title: Encode design intent as assertions
description: Capture protocol and invariant expectations as SystemVerilog assertions so violations fail at the cycle they occur, not in downstream waveforms.
body: Use when verifying control logic, handshakes, or interface protocols. Do not rely on eyeballing waveforms or only checking final outputs. Actions: write immediate assertions for combinational invariants and concurrent (property/sequence) assertions for temporal protocols (e.g. req must be followed by ack within N cycles); bind them to the DUT. Validation signals include assertion pass/fail counts in the sim log and coverage of each protocol rule. Risk boundary: an interface change without updated assertions can pass tests yet violate the protocol; require assertion review on protocol edits.
